methods.method_name: Pretreatment and Measurement of d13C Diatom
methods.description: This method involves the pretreatment of diatom samples to remove inorganic and organic carbon, followed by the measurement of d13C ratios. The process ensures that only the carbon within the diatom frustules is analyzed, eliminating external carbon sources.

steps.description: All samples were first passed through a 1-mm gauge sieve then heated to 70˚C in 10% HCl for 2 h to remove inorganic carbon.
steps.searchable_summary: sieve samples and heat in HCl to remove inorganic carbon

steps.description: For the determination of d13C diatom, organic carbon was removed through oxidation by heating samples in 30% H2O2 for 15 h at 70˚C and a further 2 h at 100˚C.
steps.searchable_summary: oxidize samples with H2O2 to remove organic carbon

steps.description: Persistent organic carbon was eliminated through heating samples to 70˚C for 1 h in concentrated HNO3.
steps.searchable_summary: heat samples in HNO3 to remove persistent organic carbon
outputs: samples with removed persistent organic carbon

steps.description: Large mineral grains were separated by differential separation and discarded.
steps.searchable_summary: separate and discard large mineral grains

steps.description: Clay and silt particles with similar densities to diatoms were reduced by sieving to 20 mm.
steps.searchable_summary: sieve samples to reduce clay and silt particles
outputs: samples with reduced clay and silt particles

steps.description: 13C/12C ratios for diatom material and bulk organic material from sediments were determined using an Elementar vario PYRO cube elemental analyser linked to an Iso-Prime100 isotope ratio mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: measure 13C/12C ratios using isotope ratio mass spectrometer

methods.description: This method involves the analysis of water samples to measure pH, electrical conductivity, total phosphorus, total dissolved nitrogen, and d13C of the DIC pool. These measurements help in understanding the chemical composition of water bodies.

steps.description: In-stream spot measurements of river site pH and electrical conductivity (EC) were taken using a WTW Multi 340i multiparameter water meter.
steps.searchable_summary: measure pH and EC using multiparameter water meter

steps.description: Total phosphorus (TP) was measured following an acid-persulphate digest using a Seal Analytical AQ2þ discrete colorimetric analyser.
steps.searchable_summary: measure total phosphorus using colorimetric analyser

steps.description: Total dissolved nitrogen (TDN) was measured using an Analytical Sciences Thermalox analyser.
steps.searchable_summary: measure total dissolved nitrogen using Thermalox analyser

steps.description: For determination of d13C of the DIC pool, 10 mL of river water was injected into 12 mL preevacuated exetainers containing 150 mL of de-gassed, concentrated phosphoric acid.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare samples for d13C DIC measurement
outputs: prepared samples for d13C DIC measurement

steps.description: d13C values of the product CO2 were measured using a GV Instruments Tracegas Pre-concentrator coupled to an IsoPrime isotope ratio mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: measure d13C values using isotope ratio mass spectrometer
